chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. No. S-1421 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________ HEARING / PRIORITY 1) For orders as to non prosecution of CMA No. 83/2026. 2) For orders on office objection No. 1 to 5 a/w reply as at “A”. 3) For hearing of CMA No. 9044/2025. 4) For hearing of main case. 11.02.2026. Mr. Nadir Khan Burdi, Advocate for Petitioners. Mr. Ali Raza, APG. Mr. Kamran Ahmed Khan, AAG. Mr. Mian Haad A. M. Paggawala, Advocate for Respondent No. 8. Inspector Ziyad Ali, SHO Thatta. SIP Muhammad Nisar, P.S. Preedy South Karachi. ___________________ Instant Petition has been filed by the Petitioner seeking protection from what they claim are false FIRs and continued harassment in the noted FIRs. Learned Counsel appearing for the Petitioner further submits that directions may be passed against Respondents not to lodge any further FIRs against the Petitioners and all the FIRs which have been lodged are result of personal vendetta against the Petitioners. Learned Counsel for the Petitioners has further relied upon the Judgment reported in the case of Jam Saqi and another v. Province of Sindh through Secretary, Home Department, Karachi and 6 others1. With respect, the facts of the noted Petition are different from the cited Judgment as the same pertained to political victimization of the Petitioner. Conversely, learned Counsel appearing for Respondent No. 8 has argued that he is complainant in FIR No. 344 of 2025 at P.S. Thatta. He has categorically stated that the Petitioners before this Court have not been implicated in the noted FIR neither their names are appearing in the challan 1 (2005 P Cr L J 1626).

Thatta. He has categorically stated that the Petitioners before this Court have not been implicated in the noted FIR neither their names are appearing in the challan 1 (2005 P Cr L J 1626). 2 filed before the trial Court in the instant FIR. In respect of other FIRs, he has submitted that he is not the complainant in the same and therefore, has refrained himself from making any submissions in that regard. In light of what have been contended by both the learned Counsels, the instant Petition is disposed of with directions to the Respondents to ensure that protection is given to the above noted Petitioners and no undue harassment to be caused to them. Petition is disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Arshad/
